title: Mnazi Bay - Ruvuma Estuary (Marine Park Villages) Regulations, 2006
title_en: Mnazi Bay - Ruvuma Estuary (Marine Park Villages) Regulations, 2006

provisions.title: Citation and commencement
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Mnazi Bay - Ruvuma Estuary (Marine Park Villages) Regulations, 2006 and shall come into operation on the day of publication.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations:— “ Act ” means the Marine Parks and Reserves Act ; [Cap. 146] “ marine park ” means the Mnazi - Bay Ruvuma Estuary Marine Park; “ marine park village ” means any village or sub-village which significantly affects or is significantly affected by the Mnazi Bay - Ruvuma Estuary Marine Park.

provisions.title: MBREMP villages
provisions.content: (1) All villages on Mnazi Bay - Ruvuma Estuary Marine Park listed in the Schedule to these Regulations shall, from the date of coming into operation of these Regulations, deemed to be marine park villages for the purpose of these Regulations. (2) A marine park village may include one or more sub-villages of another village if the marine park boundary includes such sub-village or village which the activity of its residents significantly affects or depends on the marine park . (3) The Minister shall, after consultation with ther Board, determine the criteria for the inclusion of a Part or Part of a village into a marine park village . (4) The Minister may, by order published in the Gazette , declare any inhabited area within Mnazi Bay - Ruvuma Estuary Marine Park to be a marine park village for the purposes of these Regulations, and may amend the total number or the extent of the villages listed in the Schedule to these Regulations, subject to the provisions of this Regulation, (5) Clear and easily recognizable boundaries for the villages within the Mnazi Bay - Ruvuma Estuary Marine Park shall be placed and maintained; and appropriate maps or sketches indicating such boundaries shall be kept by the Warden. (6) Any person who damages, defaces, alters, shifts, removes or in anyway whatsoever interferes with any beacon, mark, fence or a notice board indicating the boundaries of the villages in the Mnazi Bay - Ruvuma Estuary Marine Park, commits an offence and shall, on conviction, be liable to a fine of one hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term of six months, or to both. (7) Consultation with village councils Adequate consultations with the village council affected, relevant public authorities and any other stakeholder shall be made by the Warden or other Mnazi Bay - Ruvuma Estuary Marine Park authorities in the exercise of their functions under sub-regulation (3) of regulation 3 . Schedule (Made under Regulation 3(4)) (Marine Park Villages - Mnazi Bay - Ruvuma Estuary Marine Park) Villages 1. Mkubiru 2. Nalingu 3. Mngoji 4. Msimbati 5. Litembe 6. Tangazo 7. Kilambo 8. Mitambo 9. Madimba 10. Kihimika 11. Kitunguli 12.
